steps.title: Test the first equation
steps.content: Multiply the original equation by $1.5$: $1.5(7x+5y=2)$ gives $10.5x+7.5y=3$. So equation I is the same line as the original equation, giving infinitely many solutions.

steps.title: Test the second equation
steps.content:
The original equation has slope $-\frac{7}{5}$. Equation II can be rewritten as
$$
y=\frac{7}{5}x-\frac{2}{5}
$$
The slopes are different, so these two lines intersect in exactly one solution.

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graphing the original equation with equations I and II shows that equation I overlaps the original line, while equation II crosses it once. Both equations could be the other equation in a system with at least one solution. The answer is C.
